data.text: Using the `ddx` workflow for this bead. I’m going to inspect the targeted cost path, confirm the scope against the acceptance criteria, then patch and test only what is needed.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "ddx bead show ddx-a8718bec && printf '\\n---\\n' && git status --short && printf '\\n---\\n' && rg -n \"max-cost|review-cost-deferred|CostUSD|Fizeau|reviewer\" -S ."

data.output:
ID:       ddx-a8718bec
Title:    cost: charge reviewer cost to cap; review-cost-deferred policy
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 3
Labels:   phase:2, story:10, area:cost, kind:fix
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     Today reviewer cost is silently undercounted (the review path doesn't accumulate CostUSD into --max-cost). CostUSD is ALREADY reported by Fizeau on Final events for every call (no upstream change needed — it's been there). Fix: charge reviewer cost to --max-cost on the same accumulator the implementer uses. Policy: when implementer fits but implementer+reviewer would exceed, defer review (emit 'review-cost-deferred' event) rather than silently skip.
Accept:   1. --max-cost includes reviewer cost in the accumulator. 2. review-cost-deferred event when budget would be exceeded by review. 3. Tests cover the deferral case + the normal fit case.

./CLAUDE.md:216:When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:33:{"id":"corpus-2205629c24fa","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-264e7ea0","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one correlated session using route-target `qwen/qwen3-coder-30b` and `cost_usd=0` prints `state=estimated cost=0` from `processmetrics.New(dir).Cost(...)` instead of `state=known cost=0`.\nSuggested fix: Treat `cost_usd \u003e= 0` as known, handle the `-1` sentinel explicitly, and add regression coverage for zero-cost local sessions in both cost rows and summary counters.","acceptance":"A session row with cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known, contributes to known-cost counts instead of estimated-cost counts, the -1 sentinel still maps to unknown, and regression tests cover zero-cost + sentinel cases","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:api"],"bead":{"id":"hx-2ab700af","title":"correctness: classify zero-cost sessions as known in process metrics","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1083\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `sessionCostState` and `sessionDerivedCost` treat any non-positive `cost_usd` as not-known by checking `sess.CostUSD \u003e 0`. SD-016 explicitly says known-zero values are valid and must remain `cost_state=known`; only the `-1` sentinel means unknown. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one correlated session using route-target `qwen/qwen3-coder-30b` and `cost_usd=0` prints `state=estimated cost=0` from `processmetrics.New(dir).Cost(...)` instead of `state=known cost=0`.\nSuggested fix: Treat `cost_usd \u003e= 0` as known, handle the `-1` sentinel explicitly, and add regression coverage for zero-cost local sessions in both cost rows and summary counters.","acceptance":"A session row with cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known, contributes to known-cost counts instead of estimated-cost counts, the -1 sentinel still maps to unknown, and regression tests cover zero-cost + sentinel cases","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:api"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:166:{"id":"corpus-b7113543d6ca","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Data route-target\n\nAdd to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:\n\n- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.\n- `CostSource string` — `\"catalog\" | \"subscription\" | \"unknown\" | \"user-config\"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.\n\nPopulate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:\n\n- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:\n  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).\n  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).\n  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).\n  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.\n  - `CostSource: \"subscription\"`.\n- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: \"unknown\"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.\n\n### Scoring changes\n\n`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:\n1. Eligible\n2. Quota OK / healthy\n3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged\n4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new\n5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)\n6. Name\n\nFor unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: \"unknown\"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.\n\n### Unknown-cost policy\n\nA documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: \"unknown\"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.\n\n## Subscription-quota pricing curve\n\nDefault curve (tunable):\n- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0\n- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target\n- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token\n- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)","acceptance":"1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.\n2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.\n3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.\n4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).\n5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.\n6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.\n8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.\n9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).\n10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.","labels":["routing","cost","routing-plan"],"bead":{"id":"agent-0dafc7f0","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"execute-loop\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-05b4cc9d","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:246:{"id":"corpus-5078ed4eda9b","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"telemetry: emit override and rejected_override events from Execute","description":"Bead 1 of ADR-006. Emit override and rejected_override events from Execute so the routing-quality signal becomes measurable.\n\nWhen ExecuteRequest has any of Runner, Upstream-service, or Route-target set, the service:\n1. Runs ResolveRoute as today, producing the constrained decision (the dispatched candidate).\n2. Runs a second ResolveRoute with the override axes stripped, producing the unconstrained auto decision.\n3. Emits an 'override' event before the final event with the payload shape from ADR-006 §3 (user_pin, auto_decision, axes_overridden, match_per_axis, auto_score, auto_components, prompt_features, reason_hint, outcome).\n4. Outcome is populated post-execution from the existing final-event status fields.\n\nWhen a pin fails pre-dispatch (orphan route-target, unknown upstream-service, runner/upstream-service incompatibility), emit a 'rejected_override' event with the same shape minus outcome.\n\nprompt_features.estimated_tokens uses ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens if provided, else best-effort runner-level tokenizer (nullable).\n\nreason_hint accepts a free-form string from Metadata[\"override.reason\"] (programmatic) or a future --override-reason CLI flag.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- service.go (event payload struct definitions)\n- service_events.go (event type constants + decoded event mapping)\n- service_execute.go (override capture + event emission, second ResolveRoute call)\n- service_execute_test.go (event-shape and emission-order tests)\n\nOut of scope:\n- RouteStatus / UsageReport metric exposure (bead 2)\n- CLI surface (bead 3)\n- Any change to existing routing-decision events\n- Automatic learning loop","acceptance":"1. ExecuteRequest with no override axes set produces no override event (verified in TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest).\n2. ExecuteRequest with Runner, Upstream-service, or Route-target set produces exactly one override event before the final event. Verified in TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal across all three axes individually and in combination.\n3. The override event's auto_decision field is computed from a second ResolveRoute call with the override axes stripped. When pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires (coincidental-agreement case). Verified in TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement.\n4. axes_overridden lists exactly the axes the caller pinned (subset of {runner, upstream-service, route-target}). Verified in TestOverrideEventAxesOverriddenIsExplicit.\n5. Outcome is populated post-execution with status, cost_usd, duration_ms matching the final event's values. Verified in TestOverrideEventOutcomePopulatedFromFinal.\n6. Pre-dispatch failure of a pin (orphan route-target, unknown upstream-service) produces a rejected_override event and no override event. Verified in TestRejectedOverrideEventOnOrphanRoute-target.\n7. prompt_features.estimated_tokens reflects ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens when set; nullable otherwise. Verified in TestOverrideEventPromptFeaturesPopulation.\n8. reason_hint reflects Metadata[\"override.reason\"] if set; empty string otherwise.\n9. go test ./... -count=1 passes.","labels":["area:service","area:telemetry","kind:feature"],"bead":{"id":"agent-9fc2633c","title":"telemetry: emit override and rejected_override events from Execute","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Bead 1 of ADR-006. Runs ResolveRoute as today, producing the constrained decision (the dispatched candidate).\n2. Runs a second ResolveRoute with the override axes stripped, producing the unconstrained auto decision.\n3. Emits an 'override' event before the final event with the payload shape from ADR-006 §3 (user_pin, auto_decision, axes_overridden, match_per_axis, auto_score, auto_components, prompt_features, reason_hint, outcome).\n4. Outcome is populated post-execution from the existing final-event status fields.\n\nWhen a pin fails pre-dispatch (orphan route-target, unknown upstream-service, runner/upstream-service incompatibility), emit a 'rejected_override' event with the same shape minus outcome.\n\nprompt_features.estimated_tokens uses ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens if provided, else best-effort runner-level tokenizer (nullable).\n\nreason_hint accepts a free-form string from Metadata[\"override.reason\"] (programmatic) or a future --override-reason CLI flag.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- service.go (event payload struct definitions)\n- service_events.go (event type constants + decoded event mapping)\n- service_execute.go (override capture + event emission, second ResolveRoute call)\n- service_execute_test.go (event-shape and emission-order tests)\n\nOut of scope:\n- RouteStatus / UsageReport metric exposure (bead 2)\n- CLI surface (bead 3)\n- Any change to existing routing-decision events\n- Automatic learning loop","acceptance":"1. ExecuteRequest with no override axes set produces no override event (verified in TestExecuteEmitsNoOverrideEventForUnpinnedRequest).\n2. ExecuteRequest with Runner, Upstream-service, or Route-target set produces exactly one override event before the final event. Verified in TestExecuteEmitsOverrideEventBeforeFinal across all three axes individually and in combination.\n3. The override event's auto_decision field is computed from a second ResolveRoute call with the override axes stripped. When pinning matches what unconstrained auto would produce, match_per_axis values are true; the event still fires (coincidental-agreement case). Verified in TestOverrideEventCoincidentalAgreement.\n4. axes_overridden lists exactly the axes the caller pinned (subset of {runner, upstream-service, route-target}). Verified in TestOverrideEventAxesOverriddenIsExplicit.\n5. Outcome is populated post-execution with status, cost_usd, duration_ms matching the final event's values. Verified in TestOverrideEventOutcomePopulatedFromFinal.\n6. Pre-dispatch failure of a pin (orphan route-target, unknown upstream-service) produces a rejected_override event and no override event. Verified in TestRejectedOverrideEventOnOrphanRoute-target.\n7. prompt_features.estimated_tokens reflects ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens when set; nullable otherwise. Verified in TestOverrideEventPromptFeaturesPopulation.\n8. reason_hint reflects Metadata[\"override.reason\"] if set; empty string otherwise.\n9. go test ./... -count=1 passes.","labels":["area:service","area:telemetry","kind:feature"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:251:{"id":"corpus-926b27e7332a","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request","description":"## Observed\n\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(\"\", \"\", \"\", \"\")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.\n\nSo every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.\n\n`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.\n\n## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)\n\nAny of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:\n\n1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.\n2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.\n3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.\n\nBefore picking: add a baseline measurement runner (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.","acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. A benchmark/runner exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.\n2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).\n3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.\n4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).\n5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.\n6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-ad0db8fd","title":"Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:259:{"id":"corpus-ef0f9a2702f5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","labels":["area:agent","area:runner","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. (a) What is done
----------------
AC #1 — 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip (ort
strategy + -X ours heuristic) before discarding:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:845 landConflictAutoRecover(...)
  Uses `git merge --no-ff -s ort -X ours` in a temp worktree at currentTip,
  aborts cleanly on failure, advances refs/heads/<target> via update-ref +
  SyncWorkTreeToHead on success.

AC #2 — On 3-way failure, dispatch a focused conflict-resolve agent:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:279 ExecuteBeadWorker.ConflictResolver
  func(ctx, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
  Invoked at execute_bead_loop.go:1450 inside runConflictRecovery; on success
  the merge is committed and the bead lands.

AC #3 — Park outcome distinguishes unresolvable vs needs-human:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:264 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:270 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1467 sets NeedsHuman when
    ConflictResolver returns isBlocking=true; else Unresolvable.
  Events emitted: kind:land-conflict-unresolvable / kind:land-conflict-needs-human
  (execute_bead_loop.go:1490-1501).

AC #4 — Cooldown 5-30 min, capped under the 24h push-race cap:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:64
    const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
  Applied at execute_bead_loop.go:824 (parkUntil = now + LandConflictCooldown);
  capLoopCooldown still bounds the value under 24h.

AC #5 — Same code path runs for execution_failed-with-preserved-commit (timeout):
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1397 shouldAttemptConflictRecovery
  Returns true for ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict OR
  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed when BaseRev != ResultRev and a PreserveRef
  exists. Verified by TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery.

AC #6 — Regression tests (all pass under `go test ./internal/agent/`):
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_conflict_recover_test.go
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverySucceeds_BeadCloses (mechanical)
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverFails_EscalatesResolver (structural)
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_BlockingResolver_NeedsHuman
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_NoPreserveRef_SkipsRecovery
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_EmptyProjectRoot_SkipsRecovery

AC #7 — CHANGELOG entry: present (search "execute-loop: conflict-recovery for
preserved iterations before discard" in CHANGELOG.md).

AC #8 — FEAT-006 updated:
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:76-96 lists the new
  structured outcomes land_conflict_unresolvable / land_conflict_needs_human
  and the 3-way → focused-resolve → park escalation ladder.

Verification command run inside this worktree:
  go test ./internal/agent/ -run \

    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.031s
The full agent test suite also passes; the only failure is
TestIntegration_OpencodeEcho (unrelated, pre-existing 60s harness timeout).

(b) What is blocking
--------------------
Nothing implementation-wise is blocking. The bead's <notes> review block
("Diff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes")
appears to refer to a prior attempt whose merge commit carried no code
because the actual implementation had already landed in an earlier attempt
on main. The current base revision (ee729c4a) already contains all the
code, tests, and docs required by the AC. Producing a synthetic edit just
to satisfy the "must commit" expectation would either duplicate or
deteriorate the existing implementation.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
---------------------------------------
A follow-up attempt should:
  1. Confirm via `git log --oneline -- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go
     cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
     cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_conflict_recover_test.go` that the
     implementation commits (predating ee729c4a) are reachable from main.
  2. If they are reachable, close ddx-0097af14 directly with a
     closing_commit_sha pointing at the most recent of those implementation
     commits — no new attempt is needed.
  3. If reviewers want stronger evidence than the existing tests provide
     (e.g. an end-to-end test against a real git repo demonstrating ort
     auto-resolution of a real positional drift conflict), open a new bead
     scoped to that test rather than re-running this one.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================

    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

Both commits are ancestors of HEAD (a21580e1):
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 818b5c9e HEAD  # true
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 5f234aad HEAD  # true

The files in the worktree already contain the fix and the tests. Running
the tests shows they pass. Re-committing the same changes would produce
an empty diff or duplicate history. A well-justified no-changes is the
correct outcome here; the bead should be closed by the reviewer on the
basis of the evidence already in main.

  # expect the id→path resolution in GetWorkersGraphQL

  cd cli && go test -run TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID ./internal/server/ -v
  # expect PASS

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f4014bd5/events.jsonl:1:{"actor":"opus-reviewer","body":"Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.

1. StateProvider interface split
   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.

2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)
   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64("cursor:<slice-index>"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.

3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types
   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.

4. r.State == nil nil-check
   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.

5. node(id) prefix dispatch
   Current: node(id) switches on "node-"/"proj-" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.

None of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:44:50.987280017Z","kind":"note","source":"Stage 2.2 opus review shape concerns — propagated to 2.3","summary":"Five load-bearing decisions from Stage 2.2 review that Stage 2.3 should lock in before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7"}

.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T194516-abccd41c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-e1723c99/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-e1723c99 — no code changes required this pass.

State of CommandPalette.svelte at base-rev aeaa8f1b
---------------------------------------------------
All three AC items are already satisfied in the working tree:

1. Search input border (line 505)
   Current: class="... border-0 bg-transparent ... focus:ring-1 focus:ring-accent-lever
                  dark:focus:ring-dark-accent-lever ..."
   AC: input should drop the redundant inner border and use focus-ring-only styling.
   Status: matches AC exactly. Outer wrapper (line 498) carries the only border
   (border-b border-border-line); inner input has border-0 + focus ring.

2. kbd background (line 509)
   Current: class="rounded-none border border-border-line bg-bg-surface ...
                  dark:border-dark-border-line dark:bg-dark-bg-surface ..."
   AC: kbd gets bg-bg-surface / dark:bg-dark-bg-surface.
   Status: present.

3. Hover/selected depth pattern (lines 532, 540)
   Current: hover:bg-bg-canvas data-[selected]:bg-bg-canvas
            dark:hover:bg-dark-bg-canvas dark:data-[selected]:bg-dark-bg-canvas
   AC: hover/selected depth pattern documented and consistent with sidebar nav
   (both should use canvas for hover).
   Status: a documenting comment sits above Command.GroupItems (line 532), and
   both states resolve to bg-bg-canvas — consistent with NavShell.svelte, which
   uses hover:bg-bg-canvas across its nav rows.

Why this bead came back with REVIEW:BLOCK
-----------------------------------------
The file change actually landed in commit 4e078e38
("fix(ui): align CommandPalette tokens with sidebar nav pattern [ddx-e1723c99]"),
which is an ancestor of base-rev aeaa8f1b. The reviewer flagged the bead
because the *most recent* execute attempt's reviewable diff contained only
a result.json execution artifact — the .svelte edits were not in that
particular attempt's diff because they had already been merged in a prior
attempt. The state of the source file on disk, however, is correct.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing in the source. If the orchestrator's review step is comparing the
attempt's diff (rather than the file state) against the AC, this bead will
keep re-queuing indefinitely. Two options:

  a) Mark the bead resolved by reference to commit 4e078e38, since the
     three AC items are observably present in the source.
  b) Adjust the review gate so it inspects file state at HEAD, not just
     the diff produced by the latest execute-bead attempt.

Producing a synthetic edit-and-revert just to populate a non-empty diff
would be dishonest and would muddy the file's history; not doing that.

   (border-b border-border-line); inner input has border-0 + focus ring.

2. kbd background (line 509)
   Current: class="rounded-none border border-border-line bg-bg-surface ...
                  dark:border-dark-border-line dark:bg-dark-bg-surface ..."
   AC: kbd gets bg-bg-surface / dark:bg-dark-bg-surface.
   Status: present.

3. Hover/selected depth pattern (lines 532, 540)
   Current: hover:bg-bg-canvas data-[selected]:bg-bg-canvas
            dark:hover:bg-dark-bg-canvas dark:data-[selected]:bg-dark-bg-canvas
   AC: hover/selected depth pattern documented and consistent with sidebar nav
   (both should use canvas for hover).
   Status: a documenting comment sits above Command.GroupItems (line 532), and
   both states resolve to bg-bg-canvas — consistent with NavShell.svelte, which
   uses hover:bg-bg-canvas across its nav rows.

Why this bead came back with REVIEW:BLOCK
-----------------------------------------
The file change actually landed in commit 4e078e38
("fix(ui): align CommandPalette tokens with sidebar nav pattern [ddx-e1723c99]"),
which is an ancestor of base-rev aeaa8f1b. The reviewer flagged the bead
because the *most recent* execute attempt's reviewable diff contained only
a result.json execution artifact — the .svelte edits were not in that
particular attempt's diff because they had already been merged in a prior
attempt. The state of the source file on disk, however, is correct.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Nothing in the source. If the orchestrator's review step is comparing the
attempt's diff (rather than the file state) against the AC, this bead will
keep re-queuing indefinitely. Two options:

  a) Mark the bead resolved by reference to commit 4e078e38, since the
     three AC items are observably present in the source.
  b) Adjust the review gate so it inspects file state at HEAD, not just
     the diff produced by the latest execute-bead attempt.

Producing a synthetic edit-and-revert just to populate a non-empty diff
would be dishonest and would muddy the file's history; not doing that.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-dc2249e9/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-dc2249e9 — no_changes rationale

What is done
============
The acceptance criterion is already satisfied at the base revision
(d01bc4453e9913335945ea1461485b5c0516a5bb).

  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/executions.spec.ts:362-406

contains the test:

  test('execution detail renders cost tier information', async ({ page }) => { ... })

It loads the execution detail page (/nodes/.../executions/<id>), routes the
NodeInfo / Projects / ExecutionDetail GraphQL operations, asserts the heading
matches the execution id, and asserts the Cost quick-fact cell is present and
renders "$0.0400" — the value fmtCost(0.04) produces from blockExec.costUsd.

The Cost quick-fact cell is rendered unconditionally by the detail page at
cli/.../executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte:188-189 via fmtCost (defined at
:127-130 as `$${c.toFixed(4)}`), so the assertion is grounded in the actual UI.

This test was added in commit 472d4aaf
("test(e2e): verify cost tier info renders in execution detail [ddx-dc2249e9]")
and merged into main via 4bab0a16. It is present on the current base revision.

Why no new commit
=================
The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no test file changes are present") referred to the most recent
attempt (20260501T035858), which produced only an evidence artifact because
the test had already landed in 472d4aaf. The bead was re-queued, but the AC is
already met on main — there is no further test code to add or change in this
worktree without duplicating the existing test.

Adding a duplicate or trivially-renamed test would be churn, not progress.
The existing test fully covers the acceptance criterion: it lives in
executions.spec.ts, verifies cost-tier information is rendered in the
execution detail view, and is wired into the same Playwright suite that runs
in CI.

What a follow-up would need
===========================
If the reviewer believes the existing test is insufficient, the follow-up
should specify which additional dimension is missing — e.g. asserting on a
distinct MinPower/MaxPower field that does not yet exist in the UI, or
asserting on the Cost cell from the execution-list row rather than the
detail view. As written, the AC ("MinPower/MaxPower OR cost tier
information is rendered in execution detail view; test passes in CI") is
satisfied by the cost-tier branch of the disjunction.

.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T194756-9ebad619/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T195057-b1442ad2/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T200046-f2a43493/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

When invoked with\n# --strict, also verifies the line contains a Go symbol hint when the path\n# looks like Go source.\n#\n# Cited as the CI step that prevents SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md from\n# rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDOC_PATH=\"${1:-docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md}\"\n\nif [ ! -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH — fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. If flags.Profile is set → Profile.\n287\t//  4. Else fall back to cfg.Model (same catalog resolution) or cfg.Profile.\n288\t//  5. If nothing is set → cfg.Harness becomes HarnessOverride (legacy fallback).\n289\tfunc NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest {\n290\t\tif catalog == nil {\n291\t\t\tcatalog = BuiltinCatalog\n292\t\t}\n293\t\n294\t\treq := RouteRequest{\n295\t\t\tEffort:      flags.Effort,\n296\t\t\tPermissions: flags.Permissions,\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\t// 1. Harness override constrains routing to one harness.\n300\t\t//    --provider that names a known harness (or alias like \"local\") also\n301\t\t//    pins the harness so the routing engine never falls through to a\n302\t\t//    different harness.\n303\t\tif flags.Harness != \"\" {\n304\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = flags.Harness\n305\t\t} else if flags.Provider != \"\" {\n306\t\t\tresolved := resolveHarnessAlias(flags.Provider)\n307\t\t\t// Only treat as harness override if it names a registered harness;\n308\t\t\t// otherwise it's a ddx-agent provider name (e.g. \"vidar\") and\n309\t\t\t// should not constrain harness selection.\n310\t\t\tif _, ok := builtinHarnesses[resolved]; ok {\n311\t\t\t\treq.HarnessOverride = resolved"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"3708917d-10f5-4047-87a8-eca15bf792b1","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:52.477Z"}

Returns\n65\t// empty string if no match found.\n66\tfunc (d *DiscoveryResult) FuzzyMatchModel(input string) (resolved string, fuzzy bool) {\n67\t\tif d == nil {\n68\t\t\treturn \"\", false\n69\t\t}\n70\t\treturn fuzzyMatchInPool(input, d.AllModels())\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t// fuzzyMatchInPool resolves input against a pool of model IDs."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01BpB5ybRXLsC8V3pvMEmJe9","session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"21b0e478-edd0-4d23-81e6-41a9cd63003c","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:53.316Z"}
